explanatory_strength.rationale: Filings support a croupier fee stack on Japan ETF and institutional AUM, with explicit separation of FY2025 mid-cycle owner cash (¥137.14/sh) from FY2026 peak success-fee earnings (¥317.33/sh). Inversion on performance-fee cyclicity and illiquid tape is stated, but competitive-advantage durability versus global ETF sponsors and the holdco pass-through are only partially tested.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: Core fee, AUM, EPS, and share-count facts reconcile in filing_facts_2026-07-24, but decision-grade price is unresolved (¥464 last 100-share print vs ¥757 Yahoo 2026-07-23); yuho segment detail is absent; etf_product_pipeline and net_financial_claims proof rows are judgment plugs without standalone filing bridges; executive summary still cites 53.33% at ¥464 while valuation.json and the ledger use ¥757 and 38.04%.

downside_control.rationale: Performance_fee_cycle_reserve (−¥140/sh base) quantifies peak-cycle exposure and overlap keys are clean, but ~65% success-fee mix, market-beta earnings, broken public tape, and partial moat leave permanent capital loss plausible if normalization or exit fails; no structural floor beyond recurring base fees.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Universal contract base annualized return at ¥757 is 16.94% over 7 years—near but not clearly above a ~15% hurdle and sensitive to judgment-heavy components; Lawrence synthesis 38.04% depends on 5% growth and 16× exit on mid-cycle cash; low contract case is 6.49%. Illiquidity means modeled returns may be unfundable.

claims.claim: FY2026 success fees were ¥14.316B (+53.6% YoY), roughly 65% of combined base plus success fees, while the croupier anchor uses FY2025 split-adjusted owner cash of ¥137.14/sh—not FY2026 peak ¥317.33/sh.

At ¥757 per share (Yahoo 2026-07-23), contract additive base value ¥2,264/sh implies 16.94% annualized over 7 years, while Lawrence total synthesis is 38.04%—a wide gap driven mainly by terminal-multiple and growth judgments in core_fee_engine.

etf_product_pipeline base ¥100/sh is a bounded judgment overlay without a reinvestment DCF trace despite the method label; net_financial_claims applies an opaque 8.6% book claim_share after holdco pass-through.

strongest_counter_explanation: Japan's structural equity re-rating and Simplex's Japan-first active and thematic ETF niche sustain AUM and success-fee momentum above mid-cycle, so ¥137/sh normalized owner cash is too conservative; post-split scarcity and informed holders justify ¥757, making component-value math look cheap even after peak-fee normalization.
most_important_missing_fact: A live TSE quote with depth and sustained volume confirming an investable entry price, plus yuho-level segment and holdco cash-flow bridges separating durable base fees from cyclical performance fees.
falsifiers: Two consecutive fiscal quarters with combined base plus success fees falling more than 20% year over year without a disclosed structural fee cut
Mid-cycle owner cash per share falls below ¥100 for two consecutive fiscal years
Confirmed investable market price above ¥700 with daily volume that makes the filing-grounded discount thesis untradeable or moot
specialist_findings: Within the Munger power zone for moat, inversion, and mental-model review. Competitive advantage is partial: high ROE and Japan ETF niche, but no regulatory monopoly and global sponsors can replicate products. Invert the lollapalooza: peak success-fee year plus post-split illiquid float plus judgment pipeline overlay equals a mechanical bargain that may be wrong on normalization or impossible to execute. Component audit: core_fee_engine calculation proof is arithmetically valid on the ¥137.14 anchor but terminal value dominates; overlap controls are clean and performance_fee_cycle_reserve is appropriately negative; etf_product_pipeline and net_financial_claims remain weak proof rows. Partial moat croupier at an uncertain cycle point with broken price discovery—watch, not approve, until live market and yuho close the circle-of-competence gaps.
